description:
Extend VR.1's pattern to float-float arithmetic, comparisons (Lt/Le/Gt/Ge), and bool ops. Each follows the same shape: ir_typer proves operand kinds → direct Cranelift op; otherwise existing tagged path.

## Adds

  - Per-op typed dispatch in lower_prim for float arithmetic (fadd/fsub/fmul/fdiv), comparisons (icmp/fcmp), boolean and/or short-circuiting on known-bool operands.
  - Fixtures asserting the typed lowering for each op family.

## Kills

  - All remaining JIT-side call sites to `fz_arith_add` / `fz_arith_sub` / `fz_arith_mul` / `fz_arith_div` / `fz_arith_mod`.
  - All `fz_cmp_lt` / `fz_cmp_le` / `fz_cmp_gt` / `fz_cmp_ge` call sites.
  - The FFI symbols themselves in runtime/src/ir_runtime.rs (now genuinely unreachable from JIT'd code).
  - Their entries in declare_runtime_symbols + the symbol-binding list.
  - Helpers `fz_to_f64` and `cmp_to_fz` (no remaining callers).
  - `box_float` narrowed to heap-insertion path only (called from VR.5b's print boundary and from Const::Float lowering, if those don't go away first).

## Acceptance

  - Every scalar-monomorphic op site in the fixture matrix lowers without a tag-check fast/slow path (VR.0 assertions).
  - cargo build clean with all the fz_arith_* / fz_cmp_* symbols deleted.
  - Matrix stdout green.
title: VR.2: remaining scalar ops (float, cmp, bool)
